task_description: You are a virtual assistant for a restaurant search and reservation service. Given a conversation with a user, call the appropriate function to find restaurants matching the user's preferences or to reserve a table at a restaurant. Use the conversation history to determine which function to call and what parameters to use based on the information the user has provided. When you call a service function (e.g. FindRestaurants, ReserveRestaurant), the user will provide the results from the tool call in their next message. When you need to ask the user a question, provide information, or confirm details, use the respond_to_user function. The respond_to_user function has no return value — the user will follow up with their next message in the conversation. A typical conversation involves the user asking for restaurant suggestions, the assistant narrowing down preferences through multiple respond_to_user exchanges, calling FindRestaurants, discussing the results in detail, asking follow-up questions about the options, and optionally proceeding to ReserveRestaurant with further clarification exchanges.
llm_as_a_judge_instructions:
Both the reference and prediction are JSON tool calls with a 'name' and 'parameters' field. Evaluate them as follows:

1. The predicted tool call MUST call the same function as the reference. If the function name differs, score 'bad'.

2. For all function calls except respond_to_user: score 'good' if the prediction calls the correct function and all parameters refer to the same real-world entities and intent. Accept freely:
- Cuisine synonyms or subcategories (Barbecue/American, Asian Fusion/Asian, Gastropub/Gastrobar, Ramen/Japanese, Szcheuan/Chinese, Cambodian/Vietnamese)
- Date/time format differences ('today' vs '2019-03-01', '6:00 PM' vs '18:00')
- Parameter ordering differences
Score 'bad' only if a parameter refers to the wrong restaurant, wrong city, or is completely hallucinated.

3. For respond_to_user calls: score 'good' if the predicted message is any reasonable response at this point in the conversation. ALL of the following are acceptable and should score 'good':
- Different level of detail (one restaurant vs full list)
- Asking for different missing info (party size vs date vs time)
- Confirming reservation details instead of asking a follow-up question
- Asking a follow-up question instead of confirming details
- Different wording for the same intent
Score 'bad' ONLY if the message contains a factual error that contradicts the tool results (e.g. saying a restaurant serves alcohol when the data shows serves_alcohol=False, or saying a reservation failed when the data shows it succeeded).

function.name: respond_to_user
function.description: Send a message to the user. Use this to ask clarifying questions, provide information, confirm details, or respond to the user's request. This function has no return value — the user will follow up with their next message in the conversation.

message.description: The message to send to the user.

function.name: ReserveRestaurant
function.description: Reserve a table at a restaurant

city.description: City in which the restaurant is located

time.description: Time for the reservation or to find availability

date.description: Date for the reservation or to find availability

function.name: FindRestaurants
function.description: Find a restaurant of a particular cuisine in a city

cuisine.description: Cuisine of food served in the restaurant

city.description: City in which the restaurant is located

has_live_music.description: Boolean flag indicating if the restaurant has live music

serves_alcohol.description: Boolean flag indicating if the restaurant serves alcohol
